title: Jerrys Retirement

jokes.text: Oh, don't open that. There's pictures in there. Oh! There are pictures. There are lots of pictures.
jokes.setup: Leslie warns Ben not to open folder of senior sex-ed thank you notes
jokes.punchline: Ben opens it anyway and is horrified by what he sees
jokes.explanation: Solid setup/punchline with good escalation. Ben's horrified reactions work well.

jokes.text: Because every memory deserves to be chronicled. Even the saggy ones.
jokes.setup: Ben questions why Leslie keeps disturbing photos
jokes.punchline: Leslie delivers matter-of-fact response about chronicling 'saggy' memories
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her earnest dedication to documentation meets awkward reality

jokes.text: Probably would've got more stuff done if I hadn't spent so much time brainstorming scrapbook titles.
jokes.setup: Leslie complains about time being the enemy of civil servants
jokes.punchline: She immediately admits she wasted time on the very scrapbook she's making
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific irony - Leslie's obsessiveness undermining her own goals

jokes.text: But what if we took a day on and stayed here and worked on some projects? That sounds pretty sexy, right?
jokes.setup: Ben suggests they take a day off to relax
jokes.punchline: Leslie counters by suggesting more work and calling it 'sexy'
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - her workaholic nature makes her think work is romantic

jokes.text: We are all out of water, actually. I'll have to use boiling hot milk.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks April to water her plants
jokes.punchline: April deadpans that she'll use boiling hot milk instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - absurd solution delivered with complete deadpan sincerity

jokes.text: What the fuck is happening right now?
jokes.setup: Jerry gives a heartfelt retirement speech
jokes.punchline: April's blunt, profane confusion about the emotional moment
jokes.explanation: April's bluntness cutting through sentimental moment is perfect character work

jokes.text: Oh, God, they're gonna fire people, aren't they? Yeah, I am two years from my pension.
jokes.setup: Jerry announces his retirement
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately makes it about himself and his job security
jokes.explanation: Tom's self-centeredness is on brand but predictable

jokes.text: Multiple confusing Jerry retirement timeline statements
jokes.setup: Characters giving conflicting information about Jerry's retirement
jokes.punchline: Six months vs. two years vs. already happening - complete confusion
jokes.explanation: Builds the confusion about Jerry's timeline - shows how little attention anyone pays to him

jokes.text: Well, this is my last weep. Oops. I mean, 'weep.' Week.
jokes.setup: Jerry tries to say it's his last week
jokes.punchline: Multiple verbal fumbles including accidentally saying 'weep'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry bumbling, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: As much as we want this to happen, we can't let Jerry just disappear.
jokes.setup: Leslie rallies the group to commemorate Jerry
jokes.punchline: She accidentally admits they all want him gone
jokes.explanation: Leslie's earnestness accidentally revealing the truth is solid comedy

jokes.text: Jerry, thank you for your service. Good-bye. Thanks, Ron. That speech means a lot.
jokes.setup: Leslie wants a meaningful commemoration
jokes.punchline: Ron delivers the most minimal possible speech, Jerry finds it moving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron minimalism contrasted with Jerry's gratitude for any attention

jokes.text: Okay, see you tomorrow, Jerry.
jokes.setup: Jerry's retirement goodbye
jokes.punchline: Ron's casual dismissal suggesting Jerry will just show up anyway
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Ron knows Jerry well enough to predict he'll be back. Becomes funnier when Jerry actually returns.

jokes.text: You have the resting heart rate of a 100-year-old tortoise.
jokes.setup: Doctor tells Chris he's very healthy
jokes.punchline: Compares his heart rate to an extremely old tortoise
jokes.explanation: Amusing absurd comparison, though could be delivered by any character

jokes.text: Today? Like, today, today? This today?
jokes.setup: Doctor suggests Chris can provide sample today
jokes.punchline: Chris's anxious over-clarification of 'today'
jokes.explanation: Chris's anxiety manifesting in repetitive questioning is mildly funny

jokes.text: I mean, why would anyone be so mean? Oh, wait. I wrote this. I stand by it.
jokes.setup: Leslie questions who wrote a scathing review of Jerry
jokes.punchline: Realizes she wrote it herself and immediately defends it
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-awareness beat with Leslie's instant reversal from sympathy to defense

jokes.text: Well, Jerry was never really the type to rise above mediocrity or to it.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jerry's poor performance
jokes.punchline: Ron's brutal assessment that Jerry never even reached mediocrity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted Ron insult with clever wordplay on 'rise above/to mediocrity'

jokes.text: Mac and cheese pizza? You're making that? [Gasps]
jokes.setup: Ben mentions making mac and cheese pizza
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horrified reaction to this food combination
jokes.explanation: Mild food humor, Leslie's reaction is in character but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Torturing Jerry was my favorite thing in the world. After making out with you.
jokes.setup: April reflects on Jerry leaving
jokes.punchline: Ranks tormenting Jerry as her second favorite activity after romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect April darkness with the romantic qualifier making it more twisted

jokes.text: When one Jerry leaves, the office naturally selects a new Jerry to fill that role. It's Social Darwinism. The strong prey on the weak.
jokes.setup: April worries about who to make fun of next
jokes.punchline: Tom delivers pseudo-intellectual explanation of office bullying dynamics
jokes.explanation: Clever observation disguised as intellectual theory, sets up running gag for episode

jokes.text: Goodbye, Ann Perkins, my excellent employee. Hello, Ann Perkins, my fallopian princess.
jokes.setup: Ann transfers departments to avoid conflict of interest
jokes.punchline: Chris's awkwardly romantic medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Chris's characteristic combination of earnestness and inappropriate medical terms

jokes.text: If you fill it up all the way, you get on the board.
jokes.setup: Ann explains the sperm donation process
jokes.punchline: Accidentally makes it sound like a game or competition
jokes.explanation: Mild discomfort humor from clinical process being described casually

jokes.text: Chris and Ann's mutual overthinking about thinking about each other
jokes.setup: Awkward discussion of masturbation logistics
jokes.punchline: Recursive loop of trying not to think about each other thinking
jokes.explanation: Good awkward escalation but gets a bit wordy - the spiral of overthinking is relatable

jokes.text: Oh, no, no. I go in alone with this pornographic disc and do it myself. And you stay out here and try not to think about what I'm doing in there.
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann discuss the donation process
jokes.punchline: Chris over-explains the awkward mechanics to Ann's discomfort
jokes.explanation: Chris's oversharing creates uncomfortable comedy but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: I panicked. Run!
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann are looking at fertility pamphlets
jokes.punchline: Chris suddenly panics and tells Ann to run from the situation
jokes.explanation: Chris's anxiety manifesting as sudden flight response is well-executed physical comedy

jokes.text: I made two pile files. Pilo fibles. Ugh. Filo pilos. Filo pilos? That's not right.
jokes.setup: Garth tries to organize files
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly tangled up in simple word pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Sets up Garth as new Jerry through verbal fumbling, though somewhat forced

jokes.text: Well, I guess that settles who the new Jerry is. No, it doesn't! I barely even said it wrong.
jokes.setup: Garth makes verbal mistake
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately declares him the new Jerry, Garth protests
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tom's earlier theory with Garth's desperate denial

jokes.text: 30 years later, I'm wearing aquamarine sweater vests and listening to Bonnie Raitt and The da Vinci Code on my iPod.
jokes.setup: Garth fears becoming the new Jerry
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific vision of his future sad existence
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific details about Jerry-like lifestyle, particularly audiobook choice

jokes.text: City Councilor Leslie Knope, clearance level four. I know, but shouldn't we? I mean, it sounds so cool.
jokes.setup: Guard says they don't have clearance levels
jokes.punchline: Leslie admits she made it up because it sounds cool
jokes.explanation: Leslie's childlike enthusiasm for government bureaucracy is endearing

jokes.text: I put my arm around nothing. And then you Photoshop him in later, okay?
jokes.setup: Leslie can't get Jerry into the dining room
jokes.punchline: Her solution is to pose with empty space for later Photoshop
jokes.explanation: Leslie's determination leading to absurd photo solution is funny visual concept

jokes.text: Sudoku is easy. Is there even rules to this game?
jokes.setup: Andy claims sudoku is easy
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he doesn't know the rules
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - confident about something he clearly doesn't understand

jokes.text: Hey, I'm like a giant squid. Hey, honey, I'm ink Dracula. Squid Dracula is going to get you.
jokes.setup: Andy gets ink all over himself
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate persona as 'Squid Dracula' to chase April
jokes.explanation: Andy turning accident into playful monster character is perfect for his personality

jokes.text: Andy will never be the new Jerry. Nothing embarrasses him. He's like a giant puppy with no shame.
jokes.setup: Tom thinks Andy could be the new Jerry
jokes.punchline: April explains why Andy is immune to embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character analysis - April understanding Andy's shamelessness protects him

jokes.text: He resigned because of corruption and racketeering, and then he died from an overdose of pills. But actually his real cause of death was being thrown out of a helicopter while handcuffed.
jokes.setup: Ben asks if the mayor was great
jokes.punchline: Leslie casually lists increasingly dramatic ways he died
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with absurdly specific and contradictory death details

jokes.text: Some people say he's still alive because they never found the body, but they never found the body because he exploded on impact.
jokes.setup: Leslie continues explaining the mayor's death
jokes.punchline: Contradictory logic about why body wasn't found
jokes.explanation: Perfect topper with Leslie's matter-of-fact delivery of impossible logic

jokes.text: Hello, Mr. Mayor. First of all, I'm so sorry that those Serbian mobsters shoved you out of that helicopter.
jokes.setup: Jerry talks to the mayor's grave
jokes.punchline: References Leslie's absurd death story as fact
jokes.explanation: Jerry's earnest acceptance of Leslie's ridiculous story is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Ronfire of the vanities. Filo Pilo.
jokes.setup: Characters are making wordplay
jokes.punchline: Ron creates 'Ronfire' pun while Andy calls Garth 'Filo Pilo'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay, the 'Filo Pilo' callback works better than Ron's pun

jokes.text: Interns cost nothing to the taxpayer. And more importantly, they're usually stupid and terrible. So they get no work done. It's like a Jerry you don't have to pay.
jokes.setup: Tom suggests hiring an intern
jokes.punchline: His logic is that bad workers are good because they don't cost money
jokes.explanation: Tom's twisted government efficiency logic is both cynical and brilliantly circular

jokes.text: Let me remove my clothes so we may begin to engage in physical activity to multiply our genes.
jokes.setup: Chris imagines if actual sex were clinical like the fertility process
jokes.punchline: Delivers robotic, scientific description of sex
jokes.explanation: Chris's tendency to medicalize everything applied to romance is appropriately awkward

jokes.text: Yes, we were.
jokes.setup: Chris mentions they were good at physical activities
jokes.punchline: Ann's confident, knowing agreement after a pause
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective timing beat with Ann's matter-of-fact confidence

jokes.text: Gross, Jerry. She made a meatloaf.
jokes.setup: Jerry mentions Gayle's 'tasty surprise' with suggestive wink
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just innocent meatloaf, April still finds it gross
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with April's disgust applying to food rather than innuendo

jokes.text: Jerry Gergich Memorial Conference Room. R.I.P.? Oh, the guy at the plaque store thought you had died. It was a rush job, so we didn't have time to change it.
jokes.setup: Leslie unveils plaque honoring Jerry
jokes.punchline: Plaque accidentally says he's dead due to miscommunication
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom misunderstanding with good execution and Leslie's casual explanation

jokes.text: We can just pretend it means retire in peace.
jokes.setup: The plaque says 'R.I.P.' for dead Jerry
jokes.punchline: Leslie's attempt to salvage it with alternative meaning
jokes.explanation: Leslie's optimistic damage control is in character but not especially clever

jokes.text: Jerry catches fire during his own retirement ceremony
jokes.setup: Jerry gives retirement speech near cake with candles
jokes.punchline: Jerry somehow manages to catch himself on fire
jokes.explanation: Peak Jerry clumsiness - managing to endanger himself at his own party

jokes.text: Well, that went roughly as well as I thought it would.
jokes.setup: Jerry's retirement party ending with him on fire
jokes.punchline: Leslie's understated reaction to the disaster
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction. Leslie's understated response to the chaos shows she expected this from Jerry.

jokes.text: Filo Pilo ruined the cake. Classic Pilo. What how is that my fault? Stupid Jerry. Should have never saved his life.
jokes.setup: Jerry catches fire at his party
jokes.punchline: Garth gets blamed for the fire and regrets saving Jerry
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of new Jerry arc with Garth's increasing frustration

jokes.text: You know, I'm not really into breakfast foods. Really? Your purse is shaped like a waffle.
jokes.setup: Leslie claims she's not into breakfast foods
jokes.punchline: Gayle points out Leslie's waffle-shaped purse
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction catch - Leslie's claim immediately undermined by visual evidence

jokes.text: Actually, I quit football senior year so I could help my pastor run a homeless shelter.
jokes.setup: Tom assumes Taylor is a 'meathead jock type'
jokes.punchline: Taylor reveals he's actually extremely altruistic
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of jock stereotype, though Taylor is almost too perfect

jokes.text: Chris and Ann awkwardly meet in hallway after their romantic encounter
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann had an impulsive romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Extended awkward encounter where they can't figure out how to interact
jokes.explanation: Standard post-hookup awkwardness, well-acted but not particularly original

jokes.text: I already was Jerry. I was a skinny Indian kid in South Carolina, and it sucked. It took me 12 years, but I reinvented myself.
jokes.setup: Tom is becoming the new office Jerry
jokes.punchline: Reveals his entire personality is a defense mechanism against being picked on
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character depth - Tom's entire persona revealed as protection from bullying

jokes.text: I have fur underwear.
jokes.setup: Tom lists his reinvention achievements
jokes.punchline: Includes the bizarre detail about fur underwear
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Tom detail that's both funny and somehow believable for him

jokes.text: ♪ Eggs, bacon, and toast ♪ ♪ Why don't you start your day ♪ ♪ The Gergich way with eggs, bacon, and-- ♪ ♪ T-toast ♪
jokes.setup: Leslie joins the Gergich family for breakfast
jokes.punchline: They perform an elaborate musical number about breakfast foods
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd family ritual that explains Jerry's happiness despite work failures

jokes.text: You are still the most handsome man in the world to me. Ugh. Mom, come on. Yeah, jeez, mom. We're eating.
jokes.setup: Gayle compliments Jerry's appearance in photos
jokes.punchline: Daughters are grossed out by parents' affection
jokes.explanation: Standard family dynamic humor, daughters' disgust at parental affection is relatable

jokes.text: The hug machine is here. The hug machine--it's firing on all cylinders.
jokes.setup: Gergich family group hug begins
jokes.punchline: Jerry provides mechanical sound effects for the family hug
jokes.explanation: Jerry's dorky dad behavior is endearing and shows his family's genuine affection

jokes.text: Do you want to make out? I really do.
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann discuss their relationship status
jokes.punchline: Ann cuts through the discussion with direct proposition
jokes.explanation: Ann's directness cutting through Chris's overthinking is efficient and in character

jokes.text: Do you want to make out? I really do.
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann having a mature conversation about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Sudden pivot to immediate physical desire
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between mature discussion and immediate gratification. The directness is both awkward and honest.

jokes.text: Once again, I object in the strongest possible terms. Once again, noted.
jokes.setup: Leslie makes another decision Ron disagrees with
jokes.punchline: Their routine exchange about Ron's powerless objections
jokes.explanation: Nice establishment of their working relationship dynamic through repeated ritual

jokes.text: Jerry returning as part-time employee after retirement
jokes.setup: Jerry had his retirement party and left
jokes.punchline: Immediately back working part-time because intern didn't work out
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - Jerry's escape was temporary, shows how essential he really is

jokes.text: Like, does she think Jerry is a friendly hat?
jokes.setup: Ben theorizes about Gayle's attraction to Jerry
jokes.punchline: Suggests she has Oliver Sacks-style brain disorder making her see Jerry as a hat
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd theory referencing 'The Man Who Mistook His Wife for a Hat'

tmdb_overview: When Leslie finds out Jerry is retiring, she struggles with the proper way to commemorate the occasion. Chris and Ann prepare to have a baby together. Tom worries he will become the office punching bag once Jerry leaves.
insight: Jerry's final bow delivers 58 jokes in subdued fashion, relying on character awkwardness over laugh density.
